scenarios.description: String-overload: non-empty fragment and non-null context invokes treeBuilder.parseFragment once and returns its result
scenarios.precondition: Use a stub TreeBuilder provided to Parser that on parseFragment(reader,context,baseUri,parser) returns List.of(new org.jsoup.nodes.TextNode("x",""))
scenarios.expectedResult: returns a List containing one TextNode with text 'x'
pseudocode:
Parser parser = new Parser(stubTreeBuilder); Element ctx = new org.jsoup.nodes.Element("div"); String frag="

x

"; String uri="base";
assert nodes.size() == 1; assert nodes.get(0) instanceof org.jsoup.nodes.TextNode; assert ((org.jsoup.nodes.TextNode)nodes.get(0)).text().equals("x");

scenarios.description: String-overload: fragment is null, triggers NullPointerException before parsing
pseudocode: Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); Element ctx = new org.jsoup.nodes.Element("div"); String frag = null; String uri = "base";

scenarios.description: String-overload: context is null, passes null context through to treeBuilder
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder returning empty list when context is null
pseudocode: Parser parser = new Parser(stubTreeBuilderReturningEmpty); Element ctx = null; String frag="hi"; String uri="u";

scenarios.description: String-overload: BaseUri is null, parseFragment accepts null baseUri and returns stub result
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder that records baseUri argument and returns List.of()
scenarios.expectedResult: returns an empty list and stub received null baseUri
pseudocode: Parser parser = new Parser(stubTreeBuilderRecordingArgs); Element ctx = new org.jsoup.nodes.Element("span"); String frag="<b/>"; String uri = null;

scenarios.description: String-overload: treeBuilder.parseFragment throws IOException on Reader.read, propagates as UncheckedIOException
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder whose parseFragment calls reader.read() throwing IOException to simulate I/O error
scenarios.expectedResult: throws java.io.UncheckedIOException with cause instance of IOException
pseudocode: Parser parser = new Parser(stubTreeBuilderIOException); Element ctx = new org.jsoup.nodes.Element("p"); String frag="err"; String uri="b";
throws UncheckedIOException; assert exception.getCause() instanceof IOException;

scenarios.description: Reader-overload: non-empty fragment and non-null context invokes treeBuilder.parseFragment once and returns its result
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder provided to Parser that on parseFragment(reader,context,baseUri,parser) returns List.of(new org.jsoup.nodes.Comment("c",""))
scenarios.expectedResult: returns a List containing one Comment node with data 'c'
pseudocode: Parser parser = new Parser(stubTreeBuilder); Element ctx = new org.jsoup.nodes.Element("p"); Reader r = new java.io.StringReader("<--c-->"); String uri="u";
assert nodes.size() == 1; assert nodes.get(0) instanceof org.jsoup.nodes.Comment; assert ((org.jsoup.nodes.Comment)nodes.get(0)).getData().equals("c");

scenarios.description: Reader-overload: fragment Reader is null, triggers NullPointerException before parsing
pseudocode: Parser parser = Parser.htmlParser(); Reader r = null; Element ctx = new org.jsoup.nodes.Element("p"); String uri="b";

scenarios.description: Reader-overload: context is null, passes null context and stub returns empty
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder returning empty list when context is null
pseudocode: Parser parser = new Parser(stubTreeBuilderReturningEmpty); Reader r = new java.io.StringReader("data"); Element ctx = null; String uri="u";

scenarios.description: Reader-overload: baseUri is null, stub records null and returns list
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder that records baseUri argument and returns List.of()
scenarios.expectedResult: returns an empty list and stub recorded null baseUri
pseudocode: Parser parser = new Parser(stubTreeBuilderRecordingArgs); Reader r = new java.io.StringReader("<i/>"); Element ctx = new org.jsoup.nodes.Element("i"); String uri = null;

scenarios.description: Reader-overload: treeBuilder.parseFragment throws IOException on Reader.read, propagates as UncheckedIOException
scenarios.precondition: Use stub TreeBuilder whose parseFragment calls reader.read() throwing IOException
scenarios.expectedResult: throws java.io.UncheckedIOException with cause instance of IOException
pseudocode: Parser parser = new Parser(stubTreeBuilderIOException); Reader r = new java.io.StringReader("ioerr"); Element ctx = new org.jsoup.nodes.Element("span"); String uri="b";
throws UncheckedIOException; assert exception.getCause() instanceof IOException;
